name: Five-Grain Soda Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix 3/4 cup buttermilk (or lemon juice mixed with enough milk to measure 3/4 cup) and set aside.
2. If using powdered skim milk, mix almost 2/3 cup powder with enough water to make 1 1/2 cups milk, then add the lemon juice and bring to a total of 3/4 cup.
3. Combine the dry ingredients in a large bowl: flour mixture (all types or up to 3/4 cup all-purpose), sugar, baking powder, and baking soda.
4. Cut in the softened margarine or oil until crumbly.
5. Stir in raisins if using, then add buttermilk or sour milk mix to form a soft dough.
6. Knead lightly on a floured surface until smooth.
7. Shape into a round on a greased baking sheet, flatten to about 2 1/2 inches thick, and cut a large X across the top (about 1/4 inch deep) or shape into two smaller loaves.
8. Bake at 350°F for about 1 hour, or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.
This recipe yields approximately 128 calories per serving, with 3 grams of fat, 4 grams of protein, and 22 grams of carbohydrates.
